activated_paths.prediction: A stored local agreement preference should raise production probability for the preferred value in already-observed constructional frames.
A stable local preference can provide contextual warrant for licensing and attribution in known frames, while leaving the mechanism of generalization unspecified.
The listing baseline can make a production prediction for low-frequency QNs only after a local preference has been observed or stipulated.
The listing baseline can make a production prediction for scout-selected low-frequency QNs only after a local preference has been observed or stipulated.

prediction_tests.prediction: The listing baseline should accommodate observed agreement profiles for known QN and collective frames once their local distributions are known.
prediction_tests.pass_condition: The existing filtered COCA cells can be summarized as item- or construction-specific agreement preferences without invoking controller identification.
prediction_tests.fail_condition: The accommodation fails if known cells require notional/controller machinery even to describe their local preference.
prediction_tests.evidence_note: Existing AGR COCA cells are compatible with post-hoc local listing. This is accommodation evidence only, not projective support.

prediction_tests.prediction: The listing baseline should be weak or non-committal for low-frequency animate plural QN frames unless it adds item-specific preferences after the fact.
prediction_tests.pass_condition: The baseline stays live if the new cells are too sparse or if each item shows idiosyncratic local behaviour unrelated to notional/controller diagnostics.
prediction_tests.fail_condition: The baseline loses if registered low-frequency cells generalize in the AGR-predicted direction without enough local evidence to justify independent listing.
prediction_tests.evidence_note: Direct-string COCA run in `notes/agr-coca-low-frequency-qn-listing-rival-run-2026-06-09.md` returned no matching records for all six registered cells. This is a query-design failure, not evidence for either AGR or LIST.

prediction_tests.prediction: After a denominator-only scout freezes eligible low-frequency frames, the listing baseline should remain weak unless local item preferences are independently established before agreement rows are inspected.
prediction_tests.pass_condition: The baseline stays live if the scout finds no eligible frames, if follow-up agreement rows remain too sparse, or if selected frames behave idiosyncratically.
prediction_tests.fail_condition: The baseline loses pressure if several scout-selected low-frequency frames show the AGR-predicted plural or context-conditioned profile before item-specific listing is justified.
prediction_tests.evidence_note: V2 boundary registered in `notes/agr-listing-rival-v2-discriminator-boundary-2026-06-09.md`. The denominator scout is discovery-only; no finite-agreement follow-up has been frozen or run.

cards.expected_survival_pattern: The construction-listing baseline should accommodate already-observed QN agreement profiles but remain weak on low-frequency or novel QN generalization.
cards.notes: Rival baseline registered against AGR. It deliberately collapses controller identification, notional basis, and override pattern into local agreement preferences, so it is useful as a deflationary baseline but not as a scoped agreement module.

axes.community: COCA-attested English production in already observed QN and collective agreement frames.
axes.norm_centre: Descriptive corpus-attestation frame, not prescriptive judgment.
axes.genre: Mixed COCA registers represented in the existing AGR coded rows.
axes.medium: Written corpus transcripts/texts from COCA list and KWIC rows.
axes.task_framing: Post-hoc accommodation of known production distributions.
axes.speaker_identity: Corpus speaker/writer identity as available only through COCA source metadata.

axes.community: COCA-attested English production in pre-registered low-frequency animate plural QN frames.
axes.norm_centre: Descriptive corpus-attestation frame, not prescriptive judgment.
axes.genre: Mixed COCA registers if rows are available; sparse-cell caution required.
axes.medium: Written corpus transcripts/texts from COCA list and KWIC rows.
axes.task_framing: Prospective discriminator between AGR generalization and item-specific listing.
axes.speaker_identity: Corpus speaker/writer identity as available only through COCA source metadata.

axes.community: COCA-attested English production in scout-selected low-frequency or marginal animate plural QN-like frames.
axes.norm_centre: Descriptive corpus-attestation frame, not prescriptive judgment.
axes.genre: Mixed COCA registers if eligible rows are available; sparse-cell caution required.
axes.medium: Written corpus transcripts/texts from COCA list and KWIC rows.
axes.task_framing: Prospective two-phase discriminator: denominator-only item selection followed by frozen finite-agreement confirmation.
axes.speaker_identity: Corpus speaker/writer identity as available only through COCA source metadata.
